summary: Implement Alpha playability, policy, event, and encyclopedia surfaces
acceptance_criteria: The Web client exposes a complete Alpha flow from scenario selection through one victory path using read models and command submission, without directly mutating authoritative WorldState.

Major M6 systems have non-placeholder surfaces for diplomacy, legitimacy, succession, policies, events, encyclopedia references, AI/adviser reasons, map candidate display, save/load checkpoint state, and victory/failure status.
React and client stores do not hold full WorldState, do not manage per-entity Pixi DisplayObjects, and do not duplicate sim-core rule authority.
Storybook or Playwright coverage demonstrates the core Alpha flow and verifies that no manual node battle, telemetry, server account, or arbitrary-code mod UI is introduced.
required_tests: Playwright or Storybook coverage includes Alpha start-to-victory flow, diplomacy/legitimacy/succession surfaces, policy/event choices, encyclopedia navigation, map candidate rendering, and no placeholder major-system panels.
notes: Keep client surfaces thin and read-model driven; shared interpretation belongs in protocol/client-core, not duplicated ad hoc in React views.
No Electron Node integration, desktop file bridge, telemetry, accounts, release, or commercial decision is in scope.
Manual node battle must not appear as an implementation surface.
